purpose: Provides a weighted historical comparison framework superior to single-analogue dependence for understanding the AI investment cycle
components: Internet/telecom 1996-2002 at 30% weight (capex structure, revenue validation dynamics)
Nifty Fifty 1968-1974 at 20% weight (concentration premium, institutional herding, career risk)
Conglomerate era 1960s-1980s at 15% weight (ROIC dilution despite revenue growth)
Shale 2010-2016 at 15% weight (self-funded rationalization, external price signal as catalyst)
Telecom at 10% weight (capex/revenue dynamics, but N=1 for threshold analysis)
Japan 1990s-2000s at 10% weight (slow-grind duration risk, 13-year underperformance template)
current_assessment: Composite approach favored over single primary analogue in pair_0 debate. Current cycle has features of each analogue but none dominates. The specific weights are debatable but the composite principle is well-supported. Single-analogue dependence exhibits the selective analogy deployment bias the KB itself identifies.
limitations: Specific weights are subjective and debatable
Each analogue has materially different macro backdrop
Composite approach may underweight the truly novel aspects of the current cycle
Cross-analogue phase-position averaging produces noise (50-80% range spans nearly entire plausible range)
